rationale: The citing paper uses Xiong and Bharadwaj (2013) in its conceptual development, not just in a generic literature list. It cites this work to support the claim that media/news is perceived as credible and influential information beyond firm-controlled communication, which motivates key hypothesized media effects. The paper also references it when specifying expected links between firm performance and media coverage, and again in interpreting why certain message types are more influential. These are framing and interpretation roles that materially inform the study's setup.
relation_description: The citing study builds on Xiong and Bharadwaj (2013) as prior evidence that firm-related news/media can shape stakeholder and financial-market responses. It uses that foundation to motivate and interpret a broader price-war model in which media coverage affects consumers, retailers, and investors.
